title: Polypodium malaicum
intro: Polypodium malaicum, also known as the Malaican Polypody, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-cancer properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and enhancing immune function. The bioactive constituents of Polypodium malaicum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Polypodium malaicum is commonly used to make herbal decoctions, teas, and extracts, which are often consumed to promote overall well-being and to support the treatment of various health conditions.
